name: r
description: Start R or invoke an R tool using CMD

subcommands.name: RHOME
subcommands.description: Print path to R home directory and exit

subcommands.name: CMD
subcommands.description: Invoke an R tool

subcommands.name: BATCH
subcommands.description: Batch Execution of R

args.name: infile
args.description: A file with R code to be executed

args.name: outfile
args.description: A file to which to write output. If not given, the name used is that of infile, with a possible .R extension stripped, and .Rout appended

subcommands.name: COMPILE
subcommands.description: Compile files for use with R (not available on Windows)

subcommands.name: SHLIB
subcommands.description: Compile the given source files and then link all specified object files

options.description: Remove files created during a previous run

arg.name: LIB
arg.description: Full name for the built library

options.description: Dry run; shows commands that would be used

args.name: files
args.description: The object files to be included in the shared object/DLL

subcommands.name: INSTALL
subcommands.description: Utility for installing add-on packages

options.description: Remove files created during a previous run
Install packages to library tree LIB

arg.name: LIB
arg.description: Path to the R library tree to install to

options.description: Do not use the package's configure script
Do not install HTML, LaTeX or examples help
Install R code for help examples
Do minimal install for testing purposes
Install on top of any existing installation without using a lock directory
Use a per-package lock directory (default for a single package)
Build binaries of the installed package(s)
Suppress installation of the package R code
Suppress installation of the package libs
Suppress installation of the package data
Suppress installation of the package help
Suppress installation of the package demo
Compression to be used for lazy-loading of data
Re-save data files as compactly as possible
Do not use 'keep.source' for R code
Install to a temporary directory and then move to the target directory (default)
Install directly to the target directory
Skip test of loading installed package
Do not remove installed package on error
Multi-arch by merging (from a single tarball only)
Do not read any Renviron or Rprofile files
Set arguments for the configure scripts
Set variables for the configure scripts
Set timestamp for 'Built' entry in DESCRIPTION (Unix only)

arg.name: STAMP
arg.description: A timestamp for the build (Unix only)

options.description: Attempt to build both architectures (Windows only)
Compile both architectures on 32-bit Windows (Windows only)

subcommands.name: REMOVE
subcommands.description: Remove add-on packages

options.description: The library tree to remove from

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build R packages from package sources

options.description: Do not build the PDF manual
Re-save data files as compactly as possible
Try to compact PDF files under 'inst/doc'
Type of compression to be used on tarball
Log to file 'pkg-00build.log' when processing the pkgdir with basename 'pkg'

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Check R packages from package sources

options.description: Library used for test installation of packages
Do not clean 'outdir' before using it
Do not check for code/documentation mismatches
Do not run the examples in the Rd files
Do not run code in 'tests' subdirectory
Do not produce the PDF manual
Do not run R code in vignettes nor build outputs
Do run `dontrun{}` sections in the Rd files
Do run `donttest{}` sections in the Rd files
Use `valgrind` when running examples, tests, vignettes
Command-line args to be passed to INSTALL

arg.name: args
arg.description: Command-line args to be passed to INSTALL

options.description: Look in this subdirectory for test scripts
Do not stop running tests after first error
Run checks on the package subdirectories
Select customizations similar to those used by CRAN
Do only runtime tests needed for an additional sub-architecture
Do runtime tests on all installed sub-archs
Do runtime tests only on the main sub-architecture
Run tests on all sub-archs even for packages with no compiled code
